html:
Exclusive to:

AwakeHiding

The Stagehand is a naturally spawning object in Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It spawns in a circle of 5 Roses. The Stagehand seems to have something moving under it resembling a Shadow Hand. At night, the creature's legs creep out from under the table and search for a light source in its vicinity, where it will proceed to follow said light source while playing a soft chime. The Stagehand will only be attracted to sources that produce fire, like Campfires and Torches. Once it finds a valid light source, it will sit back down. If it is currently sitting near a light source, and the player goes to sleep nearby, the Stagehand may stand back up for some reason, though it will not move. If the player gets too close to the table when it is visible at night, the creature will go back under the table. Characters have different quotes when the "creature" is standing.

The Stagehand cannot be destroyed, but hammering it 86 times (requiring 1.72 Hammers) will make it drop an End Table Blueprint (which can be dropped multiple times), used to craft the End Table. However, the count will reset if 15 seconds pass between two Hammer strikes in the same way HP of Fences, Chests and other buildings resets 15 seconds after being struck.

If the Stagehand starts burning, or if it gets close to a fire, a Shadow Hand will come out from under the tablecloth to extinguish the flames.

End Table

GenericEmptyBurntWiltedFresh LightOld Light


The End Table is a structure that looks exactly like the Stagehand, except it excludes the shadow legs and is completely static.

Unlike the Stagehand, the End Table is craftable, and can be either burnt down, turning into Ashes, or hammered to drop half of its crafting items. The player can place any kind of Flower in the vase on the table. This includes Lune Tree Blossoms, Light Bulbs, Glow Berries and Forget-Me-Lots. Doing so will grant the player 5 Sanity, but only for the first flower or when replacing a withered flower. Placed Light Bulbs and Glow Berries provide light, but will wilt after 10 days. The other flowers won't wilt unless the End Table catches fire.

There are eight variaties of Flowers, three variaties of Light Bulbs, and two varieties of Forget-Me-Lots that show up in the vase when the player decorates the structure using the corresponding items.

Prickly Stagehand

StandingSitting

The Prickly Stagehand is a natural structure generated in the world of Don't Starve Together, added in A Little Drama update. It's a Stagehand variant that appears near the stage.

Unlike the normal Stagehand, Prickly Stagehands do not move. However, if it is attacked by a player or a player is attacked by someone during the performance, then the Stagehand will start attacking them. It creates a spiked shadow arm that is cast on the surface like a shadow, which reaches out to to attack its target. Periodically, it will get up before the next attack and start walking.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Timeless Cawnival Endtable skin was originally awarded for playing Don't Starve Together during the official Midsummer Cawnival event in 2021. As of March 24, 2022, the Loyal version of this skin was available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei Points.
Distinguished
Antique Endtable
A quaint decorative table that would make your grandmother proud.
See ingame
Distinguished
Austere Endtable
A table that would be a truly elegant addition to any unkempt wilderness base.
See ingame
Distinguished
Dragonfly Endtable
An endtable inspired by the most opulent of bloodthirsty monsters.
See ingame
Timeless / Loyal
Cawnival Endtable
A small, festive table that reminds you of feathered friends.

Year of the Clockwork Knight

Only during the Event: Year of the Clockwork Knight

During the event Year of the Clockwork Knight some of stage structures will look different.

Tips

  • Due to his tool durability perk added with his rework, Wes is the most adept survivor in getting the blueprint for The End Table, being able to hit the Stagehand 85 times without completely using up one hammer.
  • The End Table can be used as an efficient light source since it can give out light for two nights with a single Glow Berry or a Light Bulb.
  • The End Table can't be destroyed by Werepigs, Hounds and most hostile creatures and even bosses, which makes it a perfect, albeit expensive way of protecting Moon Stone or any other valuable asset.
  • To get a wilted flower the End Table can be set on fire, which can be then extinguished with a Watering Can.

Trivia

  • Stagehand and End Tables were introduced in the A Little Fixer Upper update.
  • When the Stagehand was first added to the game, it could be destroyed with a Hammer for 2 Nightmare Fuel, 1 Marble, and 1 Petals.
  • In Reap What You Sow, the wilt time of flower was increased from 2.5 days to 10 days.
    • This was changed in a hotfix shortly after the release of the Host of Horrors update to only affect Light Bulbs and Glow Berries.
  • The ability to change the skin of the Stagehand with the Clean Sweeper was added in the Waterlogged update.
  • Depending on which direction second Stagehand goes, its facial expression changes due to the position of the vase.

Assets

  • Stagehand
  • Stage Stagehand
  • Stage Stagehand during Year of the Clockwork Knight event

Gallery

  • A stagehand with its legs out at dusk.
  • Stagehand at night (top left).
  • Stagehand at dusk.
  • The walking animation of a Stagehand.
  • Different flowers used to decorate End Tables.
  • A burnt End Table.
  • Second Stagehand concept art from Rhymes With Play stream.

  • Second Stagehand animation concept art from Rhymes With Play stream.
  • Second Stagehand animation concept art from Rhymes With Play stream.
  • Second Stagehand animation concept art from Rhymes With Play stream.

  • Austere Endtable in game
  • Antique Endtable in game

“Just keep your hand to yourself, alright?”
–Wilson
“Gross! I told you we shoulda burned it!”
–Willow
“GAH! Table is creepy walking fist!”
–Wolfgang
“A creature of shadow that seeks the light... how sad...”
–Wendy
“FIVE DIGITS OF TERROR”
–WX-78
“That was an underhanded trick!”
–Wickerbottom
“I really wish I hadn't seen that.”
–Woodie
“Hmph. Impressive sleight of hand.”
–Maxwell
“Keep thy hand fröm öut mine fires!”
–Wigfrid
“Definitely not a spider!”
–Webber
“I've got to hand it to you, I was startled!”
–Warly
“Ohh! Table friend!”
–Wormwood
“Shoo!”
–Winona
“Well hello there little friend.”
–Wortox
“Leeme alone, flort!!”
–Wurt
“Maybe it wants to roast marshmallows too?”
–Walter
“You'll never catch me! I've outsmarted you and your ilk at every turn!”
–Wanda
“Something's odd here, but I can't put my finger on it.”
–Wilson
“What's a weird table doing out here? Let's burn it!”
–Willow
“Wolfgang has no use for tiny table.”
–Wolfgang
“My heart aches with its beauty.”
–Wendy
“IT'S HORRIBLE, I HATE IT”
–WX-78
“Something devious is lurking in the shadows.”
–Wickerbottom
“Not my style.”
–Woodie
“This must be the work of an unseen hand.”
–Maxwell
“Merely a table.”
–Wigfrid
“Is there a spider friend rustling beneath?”
–Webber
“Oh, what a nice table setting.”
–Warly
“Too shy?”
–Wormwood
“Why's this table givin' me the creeps?”
–Winona
“I see you!”
–Wortox
“Something... weird...”
–Wurt
“Woby, what's wrong? It's just a table.”
–Walter
“I know you're hiding in there.”
–Wanda
The Stagehand is a naturally spawning object in Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It spawns in a circle of 5 Roses. The Stagehand seems to have something moving under it resembling a Shadow Hand. At night, the creature's legs creep out from under the table and search for a light source in its vicinity, where it will proceed to follow said light source while playing a soft chime. End Table[edit | edit source]

"endtable"
GenericEmptyBurntWiltedFresh LightOld Light
“A flower in a vase on a table.”
–Wilson
“Just a bunch of dumb flowers.”
–Willow
“Little flowers are weak, but look nice.”
–Wolfgang
“We pulled the flowers from their homes for our own amusement.”
–Wendy
“MY UNDERLINGS LIKE IT, DESPITE THE LACK OF FUNCTION”
–WX-78
“It's pleasant to see a little decor out here.”
–Wickerbottom
“A little bit of nature makes everything nicer.”
–Woodie
“Purely decorative.”
–Maxwell
“Tis a tribute tö Freya's beauty.”
–Wigfrid
“Looks great!”
–Webber
“I miss table settings.”
–Warly
“Ohh! Table friend!”
–Wormwood
“Pretty sure this one won't move.”
–Winona
“I never learned my table manners.”
–Wortox
“Table with ugly flower.”
–Wurt
“Is somebody decorating out here?”
–Walter
“I make it a rule not to trust fancy tables I find in the wilderness.”
–Wanda
“I should put something in there.”
–Wilson
“I'm telling you, there's a monsterhand under that thing!”
–Willow
“Puny table will not hold Wolfgang's weight.”
–Wolfgang
“The shadow equivalent of taxidermy. I like it.”
–Wendy
“WHY DID WE MAKE THIS”
–WX-78
“Ah, could use a doily, wouldn't you say?”
–Wickerbottom
“Such a beaut of a wood table shouldn't be hidden by a cloth.”
–Woodie
“An empty vessel awaiting decoration or light.”
–Maxwell
“There is nö beast beneath.”
–Wigfrid
“Reminds me of antique shopping with mother.”
–Webber
“It could use a little something.”
–Warly
“Needs friend for cup”
–Wormwood
“Sturdily built.”
–Winona
“Now now, let's set the table.”
–Wortox
“Think fish would look better in there than flower.”
–Wurt
“Hey... I think I saw something move under there.”
–Walter
“Someone forgot to put flowers in the vase. Maybe it was me...”
–Wanda
“A burnt vase on a burnt table.”
–Wilson
“Alright, alright, so I was wrong about the hand!”
–Willow
“Is burny bits.”
–Wolfgang
“Nothing lasts forever.”
–Wendy
“CARBONIZED TRASHBITS”
–WX-78
“Well, such as it is.”
–Wickerbottom
“A burnt wood table.”
–Woodie
“That was uncalled for.”
–Maxwell
“It was nö challenge at all.”
–Wigfrid
“Antiques are dumb, anyway.”
–Webber
“Should have taken it out of the oven sooner.”
–Warly
“No! NOOOOO!”
–Wormwood
“That's a shame.”
–Winona
“That was hardly necessary.”
–Wortox
“Fire lady did it, flurt!”
–Wurt
“That's how forest fires get started!”
–Walter
“That could have gone better.”
–Wanda
“Not looking too fresh.”
–Wilson
“Those need replacing.”
–Willow
“Little flowers are weaker than usual.”
–Wolfgang
“I think it's lovelier this way...”
–Wendy
“EVEN THE SIMPLEST ORGANICS DIE”
–WX-78
“A pity. Shall we find replacements?”
–Wickerbottom
“Well, that's that.”
–Woodie
“Every lovely flower must one day wilt.”
–Maxwell
“Their beauty fades.”
–Wigfrid
“They look sad.”
–Webber
“I hope Maman Angeline is eating well without me.”
–Warly
“Sick, Friend?”
–Wormwood
“That bouquet's seen better days.”
–Winona
“Tiny flower on the table... you aren't looking very stable.”
–Wortox
“Fish wouldn't get wilty like this, florp.”
–Wurt
“Those flowers are looking pretty sad.”
–Walter
“Its age is starting to show.”
–Wanda
“It's nice to have a little light.”
–Wilson
“Not as good as a fire, but at least we won't be in the dark.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang does not like dark.”
–Wolfgang
“It's illuminating... for now.”
–Wendy
“MAXIMUM LIGHT OUTPUT”
–WX-78
“The more light to read by the better, in my opinion.”
–Wickerbottom
“Aboot time we brightened this place up, eh?”
–Woodie
“A temporary reprieve from the shadow.”
–Maxwell
“The light burns bright.”
–Wigfrid
“Nice and bright!”
–Webber
“Now we can all shine a little brighter.”
–Warly
“Light Friend looks good!”
–Wormwood
“I miss lamps.”
–Winona
“A light so bright I might still see at night!”
–Wortox
“Friendly little light.”
–Wurt
“Ta-da! Light!”
–Walter
“As good as new.”
–Wanda
“Did we remember to pick up new bulbs?”
–Wilson
“We're gonna be in the dark soon.”
–Willow
“Please do not go out, little light.”
–Wolfgang
“It, too, will die.”
–Wendy
“WARNING: POWER LOW”
–WX-78
“It is Maxwell's turn to procure bulbs. I've it written down right here.”
–Wickerbottom
“We need bulbs, eh.”
–Woodie
“I believe it is Wilson's turn to fetch bulbs.”
–Maxwell
“The light wanes.”
–Wigfrid
“It's gonna go out soon.”
–Webber
“It's looking pretty dim.”
–Warly
“Sick, Light Friend?”
–Wormwood
“That's not gonna last much longer.”
–Winona
“I'll try not to pout when that light soon goes out.”
–Wortox
“Need new light.”
–Wurt
“Looks like we need to replace a bulb.”
–Walter
“Oh no, did I forget to replace the bulb again?”
–Wanda

The Prickly Stagehand is a natural structure generated in the world of Don't Starve Together, added in A Little Drama update. It's a Stagehand variant that appears near the stage.
Unlike the normal Stagehand, Prickly Stagehands do not move. However, if it is attacked by a player or a player is attacked by someone during the performance, then the Stagehand will start attacking them. It creates a spiked shadow arm that is cast on the surface like a shadow, which reaches out to to attack its target. Periodically, it will get up before the next attack and start walking.

Due to his tool durability perk added with his rework, Wes is the most adept survivor in getting the blueprint for The End Table, being able to hit the Stagehand 85 times without completely using up one hammer.
The End Table can be used as an efficient light source since it can give out light for two nights with a single Glow Berry or a Light Bulb.

When the Stagehand was first added to the game, it could be destroyed with a Hammer for 2 Nightmare Fuel, 1 Marble, and 1 Petals.

Depending on which direction second Stagehand goes, its facial expression changes due to the position of the vase.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Stagehand là một vật thể sinh sản tự nhiên trong Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong A New Reign. Nó sinh ra trong một vòng tròn gồm 5 bông hồng. Stagehand dường như có thứ gì đó chuyển động bên dưới nó giống như Shadow Hand. Vào ban đêm, chân của sinh vật bò ra từ gầm bàn và tìm kiếm nguồn sáng ở gần nó, nơi nó sẽ đi theo nguồn sáng đó trong khi phát ra một tiếng chuông nhẹ. Stagehand sẽ chỉ bị thu hút bởi những nguồn tạo ra lửa, như Lửa trại và Đuốc. Một khi nó tìm thấy nguồn sáng hợp lệ, nó sẽ ngồi xuống. Nếu nó hiện đang ngồi gần một ngọn đèn…
title: Stagehand
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{Object Infobox |name = Stagehand |image = Stagehand.png |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |canBeBurned = 1 |resources = [[File:Blueprint (rare).png|24px|link= Blueprint|End Table Blueprint]] for [[File:End Table Build.png|28px|link=Stagehand#End Table]] |perk = Searches for [[Fire]]. |shadowAligned = 1 |spawnCode = "stagehand" }} |-|Awake= {{Quotes |wilson = Just keep your hand to yourself, alright? |willow = Gross! I told you we shoulda burned it! |wolfgang = GAH! Table is creepy walking fist! |wendy = A creature of shadow that seeks the light... how sad... |wx78 = FIVE DIGITS OF TERROR |wickerbottom = That was an underhanded trick! |woodie = I really wish I hadn't seen that. |maxwell = Hmph. Impressive sleight of hand. |wigfrid = Keep thy hand fröm öut mine fires! |webber = Definitely not a spider! |winona = Shoo! |warly = I've got to hand it to you, I was startled! |wortox = Well hello there little friend. |wormwood = Ohh! Table friend! |wurt = Leeme alone, flort!! |walter = Maybe it wants to roast marshmallows too? |wanda = You'll never catch me! I've outsmarted you and your ilk at every turn! |choose = maxwell }} |-|Hiding= {{Quotes |wilson = Something's odd here, but I can't put my finger on it. |willow = What's a weird table doing out here? Let's burn it! |wolfgang = Wolfgang has no use for tiny table. |wendy = My heart aches with its beauty. |wx78 = IT'S HORRIBLE, I HATE IT |wickerbottom = Something devious is lurking in the shadows. |woodie = Not my style. |maxwell = This must be the work of an unseen hand. |wigfrid = Merely a table. |webber = Is there a spider friend rustling beneath? |winona = Why's this table givin' me the creeps? |warly = Oh, what a nice table setting. |wortox = I see you! |wormwood = Too shy? |wurt = Something... weird... |walter = Woby, what's wrong? It's just a table. |wanda = I know you're hiding in there. |choose = maxwell }} The '''Stagehand''' is a naturally spawning object in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[A New Reign]]''. It spawns in a circle of 5 [[Flower#Rose|Roses]]. The Stagehand seems to have something moving under it resembling a [[Shadow Hand]]. At night, the creature's legs creep out from under the table and search for a light source in its vicinity, where it will proceed to follow said light source while playing a soft chime. The Stagehand will only be attracted to sources that produce fire, like [[Campfire]]s and [[Torch]]es. Once it finds a valid light source, it will sit back down. If it is currently sitting near a light source, and the player goes to sleep nearby, the Stagehand may stand back up for some reason, though it will not move. If the player gets too close to the table when it is visible at night, the creature will go back under the table. Characters have different quotes when the "creature" is standing. The Stagehand cannot be destroyed, but hammering it 86 times (requiring 1.72 [[Hammer]]s) will make it drop an End Table [[Blueprint]] (which can be dropped multiple times), used to craft the End Table. However, the count will reset if 15 seconds pass between two Hammer strikes in the same way HP of [[Wood Fence|Fences]], [[Chest]]s and other buildings resets 15 seconds after being struck. If the Stagehand starts burning, or if it gets close to a fire, a Shadow Hand will come out from under the tablecloth to extinguish the flames. {{clear}} ==End Table== {{Object Infobox |name = End Table |icon=End Table.png |description = A decorative table. |spawnCode = "endtable" |image = End Table Build.png |ingredient1 = Marble |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Boards |multiplier2 = 2 |ingredient3 = Carpeted Flooring |multiplier3 = 2 |filter = {{Pic32|Decorations Filter}} {{Pic32|Structures Filter}} |tier = 11 |canBeBurned = 1}} |-|Generic= {{Quotes |wilson = A flower in a vase on a table. |willow = Just a bunch of dumb flowers. |wolfgang = Little flowers are weak, but look nice. |wendy = We pulled the flowers from their homes for our own amusement. |wx78 = MY UNDERLINGS LIKE IT, DESPITE THE LACK OF FUNCTION |wickerbottom = It's pleasant to see a little decor out here. |woodie = A little bit of nature makes everything nicer. |maxwell = Purely decorative. |wigfrid = Tis a tribute tö Freya's beauty. |webber = Looks great! |winona = Pretty sure this one won't move. |warly = I miss table settings. |wortox = I never learned my table manners. |wormwood = Ohh! Table friend! |wurt = Table with ugly flower. |walter = Is somebody decorating out here? |wanda = I make it a rule not to trust fancy tables I find in the wilderness. |choose = wolfgang }} |-|Empty= {{Quotes |wilson = I should put something in there. |willow = I'm telling you, there's a monsterhand under that thing! |wolfgang = Puny table will not hold Wolfgang's weight. |wendy = The shadow equivalent of taxidermy. I like it. |wx78 = WHY DID WE MAKE THIS |wickerbottom = Ah, could use a doily, wouldn't you say? |woodie = Such a beaut of a wood table shouldn't be hidden by a cloth. |maxwell = An empty vessel awaiting decoration or light. |wigfrid = There is nö beast beneath. |webber = Reminds me of antique shopping with mother. |winona = Sturdily built. |warly = It could use a little something. |wortox = Now now, let's set the table. |wormwood = Needs friend for cup |wurt = Think fish would look better in there than flower. |walter = Hey... I think I saw something move under there. |wanda = Someone forgot to put flowers in the vase. Maybe it was me... |choose = wilson }} |-|Burnt= {{Quotes |wilson = A burnt vase on a burnt table. |willow = Alright, alright, so I was wrong about the hand! |wolfgang = Is burny bits. |wendy = Nothing lasts forever. |wx78 = CARBONIZED TRASHBITS |wickerbottom = Well, such as it is. |woodie = A burnt wood table. |maxwell = That was uncalled for. |wigfrid = It was nö challenge at all. |webber = Antiques are dumb, anyway. |winona = That's a shame. |warly = Should have taken it out of the oven sooner. |wortox = That was hardly necessary. |wormwood = No! NOOOOO! |wurt = Fire lady did it, flurt! |walter = That's how forest fires get started! |wanda = That could have gone better. |choose = wilson }} |-|Wilted= {{Quotes |wilson = Not looking too fresh. |willow = Those need replacing. |wolfgang = Little flowers are weaker than usual. |wendy = I think it's lovelier this way... |wx78 = EVEN THE SIMPLEST ORGANICS DIE |wickerbottom = A pity. Shall we find replacements? |woodie = Well, that's that. |maxwell = Every lovely flower must one day wilt. |wigfrid = Their beauty fades. |webber = They look sad. |winona = That bouquet's seen better days. |warly = I hope Maman Angeline is eating well without me. |wortox = Tiny flower on the table... you aren't looking very stable. |wormwood = Sick, Friend? |wurt = Fish wouldn't get wilty like this, florp. |walter = Those flowers are looking pretty sad. |wanda = Its age is starting to show. |choose = wilson }} |-|Fresh Light= {{Quotes |wilson = It's nice to have a little light. |willow = Not as good as a fire, but at least we won't be in the dark. |wolfgang = Wolfgang does not like dark. |wendy = It's illuminating... for now. |wx78 = MAXIMUM LIGHT OUTPUT |wickerbottom = The more light to read by the better, in my opinion. |woodie = Aboot time we brightened this place up, eh? |maxwell = A temporary reprieve from the shadow. |wigfrid = The light burns bright. |webber = Nice and bright! |winona = I miss lamps. |warly = Now we can all shine a little brighter. |wortox = A light so bright I might still see at night! |wormwood = Light Friend looks good! |wurt = Friendly little light. |walter = Ta-da! Light! |wanda = As good as new. |choose = wilson }} |-|Old Light= {{Quotes |wilson = Did we remember to pick up new bulbs? |willow = We're gonna be in the dark soon. |wolfgang = Please do not go out, little light. |wendy = It, too, will die. |wx78 = WARNING: POWER LOW |wickerbottom = It is Maxwell's turn to procure bulbs. I've it written down right here. |woodie = We need bulbs, eh. |maxwell = I believe it is Wilson's turn to fetch bulbs. |wigfrid = The light wanes. |webber = It's gonna go out soon. |winona = That's not gonna last much longer. |warly = It's looking pretty dim. |wortox = I'll try not to pout when that light soon goes out. |wormwood = Sick, Light Friend? |wurt = Need new light. |walter = Looks like we need to replace a bulb. |wanda = Oh no, did I forget to replace the bulb again? |choose = wilson }} The '''End Table''' is a structure that looks exactly like the Stagehand, except it excludes the shadow legs and is completely static. Unlike the Stagehand, the End Table is craftable, and can be either burnt down, turning into [[Ashes]], or hammered to drop half of its crafting items. The player can place any kind of [[Flower]] in the vase on the table. This includes [[Lune Tree Blossom]]s, [[Light Bulb]]s, [[Glow Berry|Glow Berries]] and [[Forget-Me-Lots]]. Doing so will grant the player 5 [[Sanity]], but only for the first flower or when replacing a withered flower. Placed Light Bulbs and Glow Berries provide light, but will wilt after 10 days. The other flowers won't wilt unless the End Table catches fire. There are eight variaties of Flowers, three variaties of [[Light Bulb]]s, and two varieties of Forget-Me-Lots that show up in the vase when the player decorates the structure using the corresponding items. {{clear}} == Prickly Stagehand == {{Object Infobox |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |canBeBurned = 1 |name = Prickly Stagehand |image = Stagehand2.png |health = 2924 |damage = 80 |shadowAligned = 1 |spawnCode = "stageusher" }} |-|Standing= {{Quotes |wilson = Just keep your hand to yourself, alright? |willow = Gross! I told you we shoulda burned it! |wolfgang = GAH! Table is creepy walking fist! |wendy = A creature of shadow that seeks the light... how sad... |wx78 = FIVE DIGITS OF TERROR |wickerbottom = That was an underhanded trick! |woodie = I really wish I hadn't seen that. |maxwell = Hmph. Impressive sleight of hand. |wigfrid = Keep thy hand fröm öut mine fires! |webber = Definitely not a spider! |winona = Shoo! |warly = I've got to hand it to you, I was startled! |wortox = Well hello there little friend. |wormwood = Ohh! Table friend! |wurt = Leeme alone, flort!! |walter = Maybe it wants to roast marshmallows too? |wanda = You'll never catch me! I've outsmarted you and your ilk at every turn! |choose = wilson }} |-|Sitting= {{Quotes |wilson = Something's odd here, but I can't put my finger on it. |willow = What's a weird table doing out here? Let's burn it! |wolfgang = Wolfgang has no use for tiny table. |wendy = My heart aches with its beauty. |wx78 = IT'S HORRIBLE, I HATE IT |wickerbottom = Something devious is lurking in the shadows. |woodie = Not my style. |maxwell = This must be the work of an unseen hand. |wigfrid = Merely a table. |webber = Is there a spider friend rustling beneath? |winona = Why's this table givin' me the creeps? |warly = Oh, what a nice table setting. |wortox = I see you! |wormwood = Too shy? |wurt = Something... weird... |walter = Woby, what's wrong? It's just a table. |wanda = I know you're hiding in there. |choose = wilson }} The '''Prickly Stagehand''' is a natural structure generated in the world of ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', added in ''[[A Little Drama and QOL|A Little Drama]]'' update. It's a Stagehand variant that appears near the [[stage]]. Unlike the normal Stagehand, Prickly Stagehands do not move. However, if it is attacked by a player or a player is attacked by someone during the performance, then the Stagehand will start attacking them. It creates a spiked shadow arm that is cast on the surface like a shadow, which reaches out to to attack its target. Periodically, it will get up before the next attack and start walking. {{Clear}} == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#End_Table-0|Belongings}} * The Timeless '''Cawnival Endtable''' skin was originally awarded for playing ''Don't Starve Together'' during the official [[Midsummer Cawnival]] event in 2021. As of March 24, 2022, the '''Loyal''' version of this skin was available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei Points.
{{SkinInfo |image = Antique Endtable Icon.png |rarity = Distinguished |name = Antique Endtable |description = A quaint decorative table that would make your grandmother proud. |ingame = Antique Endtable.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Austere Endtable Icon.png |rarity = Distinguished |name = Austere Endtable |description = A table that would be a truly elegant addition to any unkempt wilderness base. |ingame = Austere Endtable.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Dragonfly Endtable Icon.png |rarity = Distinguished |name = Dragonfly Endtable |description = An endtable inspired by the most opulent of bloodthirsty monsters. |ingame = Dragonfly Endtable.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Cawnival Endtable Icon.png |rarity = Timeless |rarity2 = Loyal |name = Cawnival Endtable |description = A small, festive table that reminds you of feathered friends. }}
==Year of the Clockwork Knight== {{Event|Year of the Clockwork Knight}} During the event [[Year of the Clockwork Knight]] some of stage structures will look different. =={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips== *Due to his tool durability perk added with his rework, Wes is the most adept survivor in getting the blueprint for The End Table, being able to hit the Stagehand 85 times without completely using up one hammer. *The End Table can be used as an efficient light source since it can give out light for two nights with a single [[Glow Berry]] or a [[Light Bulb]]. *The End Table can't be destroyed by [[Pig|Werepigs]], [[Hound]]s and most hostile creatures and even bosses, which makes it a perfect, albeit expensive way of protecting Moon Stone or any other valuable asset. * To get a wilted flower the End Table can be set on fire, which can be then extinguished with a [[Watering Can]]. =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == *Stagehand and End Tables were introduced in the ''[[A New Reign#September 1, 2016 - A Little Fixer Upper|A Little Fixer Upper]]'' update. * When the Stagehand was first added to the game, it could be destroyed with a Hammer for 2 [[Nightmare Fuel]], 1 [[Marble]], and 1 [[Petals]]. * In ''[[Reap What You Sow]]'', the wilt time of flower was increased from 2.5 days to 10 days. ** This was changed in a hotfix shortly after the release of the ''[[Host of Horrors]]'' update to only affect Light Bulbs and Glow Berries. * The ability to change the skin of the Stagehand with the [[Clean Sweeper]] was added in the ''[[Don't Starve Together/Version History#Waterlogged Beta .28since July 26.2C 2021.29|Waterlogged]]'' update. * Depending on which direction second Stagehand goes, its facial expression changes due to the position of the vase. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Stagehand.png|Stagehand Stagehand2.png|Stage Stagehand Stagehand_(Year_of_the_Clockwork_Knight).png|Stage Stagehand during [[Year of the Clockwork Knight]] event =={{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== DST Update Fixxer-Upper.png|End Table as seen in the poster for the ''[[A New Reign#September 1, 2016 - A Little Fixer Upper|A Little Fixer Upper]]'' update. stage hand_fire.gif|A Stagehand putting out its own fire Stage hand legs out.png|A stagehand with its legs out at dusk.
